portfolio_view: Five picks spanning five distinct sectors (oil refining, pharma, specialty chemicals, private banking, power capital goods) — a defensive/cyclical mix rather than a factor bet. All show fundamentally-verified earnings drivers backed by recent public disclosures (Q4/FY26 results, order-book updates, capex milestones). Regime caveat: with these being small/mid caps in Stage-2, stagger entries and honor the mechanical stops — a broader-market pullback would hit all five together.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 8.8->14.98%; DII 2.6->1.01%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); technicals/RS 96: RS percentile 92; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP live; balance-sheet 91: deleveraging: debt 4260 -> 1964 Cr; earnings 91: qtr PAT YoY +200%; accelerating (TTM 1349% > 3y -4%)

picks.selected_because: Highest conviction on the board (77.3), RS 92, turnaround archetype with genuine deleveraging (debt 4,260 → 1,964 Cr) and PAT swing +1,349% TTM — smart-money accumulation (FII 8.8% → 15.0%) validates it.
picks.thesis: CPCL is India's Navratna PSU refiner (status granted June 2026) with FY26 revenue ₹63,640 Cr (+7.2%) and net profit ₹3,103 Cr (+1,349% YoY). Gross Refining Margins have normalized, and the Cauvery basin exploration adds optionality. Ownership by IOC + Navratna autonomy gives capex flexibility for the planned Nagapattinam expansion.
picks.catalyst: Q1 FY27 board meeting on **July 23, 2026** — earnings print is imminent (11 days from today).
picks.management: PSU under IOC parentage; recent Navratna upgrade is a governance signal — implies stronger financial discipline and autonomy.
picks.risks: (1) Refining margins are cyclical — a crude spike or spread compression could reverse the earnings tailwind fast. (2) PSU capex cycles historically overrun and dilute ROCE.
picks.watch_for: GRM compression below $6/bbl on the July 23 print.

meta.highlights: earnings 100: qtr PAT YoY +200%; accelerating (TTM 42% > 3y 38%); valuation 95: P/E 38 cheaper than growth (PEG 0.99); balance-sheet 93: deleveraging: debt 1481 -> 809 Cr; governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: Conviction 72, best PEG on the board (0.99), earnings score 100 (PAT +200% qtr, TTM 42% > 3y 38%), deleveraging (debt 1,481 → 809 Cr) — cleanest balance of all dimensions.
picks.thesis: Ipca has integrated Unichem Laboratories (33.4% stake at ₹440/sh + open offer) and management is guiding FY27 as a step-up year targeting **22-23% operating margins** and faster growth. Unichem brings 71 approved ANDAs — Ipca finally gets meaningful US generics exposure it lacked. Domestic formulations (~40% of mix) remain the anchor.
picks.catalyst: FY27 Q1 results expected late July / early August; margin trajectory post-Unichem is the litmus test.
picks.management: Long-tenured founder-led team; the Unichem deal was strategically sound though initially seen as expensive/dilutive — margin execution is now the credibility test.
picks.risks: (1) Unichem's US business shrank 2% YoY in Q3 FY26 on market-share loss — integration risk is real. (2) Any USFDA plant observation could dent sentiment (past 483 history).
picks.watch_for: Consolidated OPM slipping below 20% in FY27 quarterly prints.

meta.highlights: earnings 100: qtr PAT YoY +124%; accelerating (TTM 132% > 3y 22%); technicals/RS 95: RS percentile 88; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP live; governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); smart-money 69: FII 21.55->23.78%; DII 28.7->27.61%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: Conviction 67.6, RS 88, earnings score 100 (PAT +124% qtr, TTM 132% > 3y 22% = massive acceleration), FII adding (21.6% → 23.8%), Stage-2 with live VCP.
picks.thesis: FY26 delivered net profit ₹663.6 Cr (+130%), revenue ₹3,314 Cr (+41%), with Specialty Chemicals segment +44%. Dahej capex pipeline is the growth machine: HFC 15,000 MTPA (₹236 Cr, Q3 FY27), MPP de-bottlenecking (₹75 Cr, Q3 FY27) for a global-innovator molecule with ₹140-160 Cr peak revenue, and ₹120 Cr advanced materials by Q1 FY27. CDMO validation completed with European MSA supplies live.
picks.catalyst: MPP de-bottlenecking commissioning and new HFC capacity in Q3 FY27 — visible timeline.
picks.management: Padmanabh Mafatlal group; long track record in fluorochemistry, credible capex execution history.
picks.risks: (1) Specialty chem multiples have re-rated hard — richly valued going in. (2) A single innovator-molecule delay could stall the FY27 growth trajectory.
picks.watch_for: Any Dahej commissioning slippage past Q3 FY27.

meta.highlights: governance 95: no pledge disclosed; promoter buying; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); technicals/RS 88: RS percentile 70; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP live; smart-money 82: FII 11.69->12.64%; DII 31.79->32.8%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); catalyst 60: 9 trusted-source headlines/30d (of 10); events: approval, qip, target price; sentiment neu

picks.selected_because: Conviction 65.5, governance score 95 (promoter buying — rare and bullish), Stage-2 with VCP, smart-money supportive (FII + DII both adding). The only bank in the shortlist with a clean setup.
picks.thesis: FY26 PAT ₹732 Cr (+19% YoY), NIM improved to 3.39%, advances +18% led by mortgages, co-lending (+25%), and agri banking. **Asset quality at multi-year lows** — GNPA 2.45%, NNPA 0.89%, net slippages turned negative (-21 bps). CAR 16.55% supports growth without immediate dilution.
picks.catalyst: Q1 FY27 results (typically late July) — continuation of the asset-quality trend would trigger re-rating from current sub-1x book.
picks.management: Aga Khan Fund promoter — long-term, patient capital; conservative underwriting culture; the promoter-buying flag is a meaningful confidence signal.
picks.risks: (1) NIM peaked; further rate cuts would compress spreads. (2) SME/mortgage-heavy book is sensitive to any economic slowdown.
picks.watch_for: GNPA re-inflecting above 2.7% or NIM dropping below 3.25%.

meta.highlights: technicals/RS 97: RS percentile 94; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP live; earnings 93: qtr PAT YoY +70%; accelerating (TTM 90% > 3y 81%); governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); smart-money 54: FII 1.97->2.28%; DII 6.0->5.97%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: Conviction 65.3, RS 94, Quality + Hyper-growth archetype (rare combo), TTM PAT growth 90% > 3y 81%, Stage-2 with live VCP. The purest listed HVDC play.
picks.thesis: FY26 closed with **₹1,400+ Cr order book (1.4x revenue)** giving multi-year visibility. Landmark FY26 HVDC wins (both LCC and VSC) on Indian inter-regional links + Australian multi-terminal VSC schemes. ₹152 Cr BESS order (March 2026) opens a new vertical. **Winwin Speciality Insulators acquisition (June 10, 2026, ₹315 Cr)** adds ceramic/polymer insulators up to 1200 kV — vertical integration. India's transmission capex cycle (HVDC corridors for renewable evacuation) is a decade-long tailwind.
picks.catalyst: Q1 FY27 results + Winwin integration disclosures; further HVDC reactor orders (800 kV pipeline mentioned).
picks.management: 2001-established niche specialist; execution track record on complex HV equipment is credible.
picks.risks: (1) Rich valuation prices in the tailwind — any order slippage gets punished. (2) Winwin acquisition integration risk in a small-cap acquiring a materially-sized asset.
picks.watch_for:
Order-book decline QoQ or Winwin execution missteps.
